title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming Mercimek Corbasi, authentic Turkish red lentil soup, garnished with fresh mint and a drizzle of chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of Mercimek Corbasi, the classic Turkish red lentil soup, perfect for a cozy meal.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, a staple in every Turkish home. This comforting and nutritious soup is incredibly easy to make, offering a rich, savory flavor with a hint of mint and chili. It is the perfect heartwarming dish for any time of year, bringing a taste of authentic Anatolian cuisine to your table.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lentils
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This step is crucial for a cleaner soup.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Build the Flavor Base
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste, dried mint, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, allowing the spices to bloom and the tomato paste to deepen in color.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Liquid
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils to the pot and stir to combine with the aromatic base. Pour in 6-7 cups of water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup
instructions.text: Once the lentils are cooked, use an immersion blender to blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth. If you do not have an immersion blender, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth. Be cautious as hot liquids expand.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chili Butter (Nane Yagli Tereyag)
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Add 1 tsp of dried mint and 1/2 tsp of red pepper flakes (if using) to the melted butter. Cook for about 30 seconds, just until the mint becomes fragrant and the butter is infused, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving with a spoonful of the warm chili butter. Serve immediately with lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, which adds a refreshing tang.

tips: For an extra smooth soup, ensure you blend thoroughly. A high-speed blender will give the creamiest texture, but an immersion blender works perfectly for convenience.
The spiced butter, known as nane yagli tereyag, is a signature Turkish touch. Do not skip this step as it adds a beautiful aroma and rich flavor that elevates the soup.
Always serve Mercimek Corbasi with fresh lemon wedges. A squeeze of lemon juice brightens the flavors and adds a delightful zest, making the soup even more authentic and enjoyable.
article:
## Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup: A Taste of Turkish Comfort

In the vibrant tapestry of Turkish cuisine, certain dishes stand out not just for their flavor but for their profound cultural significance. Among these, Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved red lentil soup, holds a special place. More than just a simple soup, it's a hug in a bowl, a staple on every Turkish dinner table, and a testament to the country's rich culinary heritage. Whether served as a comforting starter, a light lunch, or a soothing remedy on a cold day, Mercimek Corbasi embodies warmth, nourishment, and a depth of flavor that belies its humble ingredients. This article will guide you through the journey of understanding, preparing, and savoring this iconic Turkish delight, ensuring you can bring the authentic taste of Turkey into your own kitchen.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup?

Mercimek Corbasi (pronounced mer-jee-MEK chor-ba-suh) is a traditional Turkish soup primarily made from red lentils. It’s renowned for its beautiful reddish-orange hue, silky-smooth texture, and a wonderfully aromatic flavor profile. Unlike some lentil soups that can be chunky, Mercimek Corbasi is typically pureed to a creamy consistency, making it incredibly comforting and easy to digest.

At its heart, this soup is a testament to the power of simple ingredients. Red lentils, which cook down quickly and completely, form the base. They are simmered with aromatic vegetables like onions and garlic, often enriched with tomato paste, and then seasoned with traditional Turkish spices, most notably dried mint and pul biber (Turkish chili flakes). The final touch is often a swirl of butter infused with more dried mint and sometimes a hint of paprika, which floats invitingly on top, adding another layer of visual appeal and flavor. This soup is not just delicious; it's also highly nutritious, packed with protein, fiber, and essential minerals, making it a wholesome choice for any meal.

## Ingredients You Need for Mercimek Corbasi

Creating an authentic Mercimek Corbasi doesn't require an extensive shopping list. The magic lies in the quality of these core ingredients and how they are brought together.

*   **Red Lentils:** Approximately 1 cup (200g). Split red lentils are preferred as they cook quickly and break down easily, creating that signature creamy texture. Ensure they are well-rinsed.
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, finely chopped. Provides a foundational sweetness and aroma.
*   **Garlic:** 2-3 cloves, minced. Adds a pungent depth of flavor.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** 2-3 tablespoons. For sautéing the aromatics and making the final drizzle.
*   **Tomato Paste:** 1 tablespoon. Contributes to the soup's color and umami depth.
*   **Dried Mint (Nane):** 1-2 teaspoons, plus extra for the garnish. This is a non-negotiable ingredient for authentic Turkish flavor.
*   **Cumin:** 1 teaspoon. Adds an earthy warmth.
*   **Pul Biber (Turkish Chili Flakes):** 1/2 - 1 teaspoon, or to taste. Provides a gentle heat and vibrant color. If unavailable, use a pinch of regular chili flakes.
*   **Water or Vegetable Broth:** 6-7 cups (1.5 - 1.75 liters). For simmering the lentils. Broth will add more flavor.
*   **Salt and Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Lemon Wedges:** For serving. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice brightens the entire soup.

Optional additions for the topping: A small knob of butter (1-2 tbsp) for melting with dried mint and a pinch of pul biber for drizzling.

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Mercimek Corbasi

Making Mercimek Corbasi is a straightforward process that yields incredibly rewarding results. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl:

1.  **Rinse the Lentils:** Place the red lentils in a fine-mesh sieve and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch. Set aside.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil (or butter) over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Spices:** Stir in the tomato paste, dried mint, cumin, and pul biber. Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, allowing the spices to bloom and the tomato paste to deepen in color. This step is crucial for developing the soup's rich flavor.
4.  **Combine Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot and stir to coat them with the aromatic mixture. Pour in the water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer.
5.  **Simmer and Cook:** Cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have mostly broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking to the bottom of the pot.
6.  **Blend the Soup:** Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a standard blender, being very cautious with hot liquids (fill only halfway, secure the lid, and blend on low, increasing speed gradually). Return the blended soup to the pot.
7.  **Adjust Consistency and Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. Simmer for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
8.  **Prepare the Mint Butter Drizzle (Optional but Recommended):** In a small pan, melt 1-2 tablespoons of butter over medium-low heat. Once melted, add 1-2 teaspoons of dried mint and a pinch of pul biber. Cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint.
9.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving with the fragrant mint butter and garnish with an extra pinch of pul biber, if desired. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side.

## Pro Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi

Achieving that perfect, authentic Mercimek Corbasi is easy with a few insider tips:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils Thoroughly:** This is key to preventing a cloudy, starchy soup and ensuring a cleaner flavor.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Tomato Paste:** Sautéing the tomato paste until it darkens slightly (but doesn't burn) caramelizes its sugars, deepening the soup's color and umami notes.
*   **Use Dried Mint:** While fresh mint is lovely for garnish, dried mint (nane) is essential for the authentic flavor profile of Turkish lentil soup. Its concentrated flavor stands up well to simmering.
*   **The Power of the Blend:** An immersion blender is your best friend here. Blend until the soup is completely smooth and creamy, with no visible lentil pieces. This is what gives Mercimek Corbasi its characteristic velvety texture.
*   **The Mint Butter Drizzle (Nane Yağı):** This final flourish is not just for looks; it adds an incredible burst of flavor and aroma. The gentle frying of mint in butter releases its full potential. Don't skip it!
*   **Fresh Lemon is a Must:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the table is absolutely crucial. It brightens the soup, cuts through its richness, and elevates all the other flavors.

## Serving Suggestions for Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways:

*   **As a Starter:** It's a classic appetizer in Turkish meals, preparing the palate for the main course.
*   **Light Meal:** Paired with crusty bread (especially Turkish pide or simit), a simple salad, and some olives, it makes a satisfying and wholesome light lunch or dinner.
*   **With Lemon and Chili:** Always serve with fresh lemon wedges on the side. A sprinkle of extra pul biber (Turkish chili flakes) allows diners to adjust the heat to their preference.
*   **Pickle Perfection:** Turkish people often enjoy their soups with a side of pickles, which provide a wonderful tangy contrast.
*   **Alongside Kebabs or Mezze:** The soup complements richer meat dishes or a spread of various mezze (small appetizers) beautifully.

## Variations and Substitutions for Mercimek Corbasi

While the classic Mercimek Corbasi is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations:

*   **Add Vegetables:** For extra nutrients and bulk, you can sauté finely diced carrots and potatoes along with the onions. They will cook down with the lentils and blend smoothly.
*   **Spice It Up:** If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of pul biber or add a pinch of cayenne pepper. You could also experiment with a tiny bit of smoked paprika for a different smoky undertone.
*   **Herb Alternatives:** While dried mint is traditional, a pinch of dried oregano or thyme can be added for a slightly different aromatic profile.
*   **Vegan Friendly:** The base recipe is already vegan if you use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and vegetable broth instead of water. For the mint drizzle, use olive oil instead of butter.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Mercimek Corbasi is naturally gluten-free, provided your broth is gluten-free.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is Mercimek Corbasi healthy?**
A: Absolutely! Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly healthy. Red lentils are a powerhouse of plant-based protein, dietary fiber, iron, and B vitamins. It's low in fat (especially if you control the oil/butter) and highly nutritious, making it an excellent choice for a balanced diet.

**Q: Can I make Mercimek Corbasi ahead of time?**
A: Yes, Mercimek Corbasi is an excellent make-ahead soup. Its flavors tend to meld and deepen overnight, often tasting even better the next day.

**Q: How do I store and reheat leftover Mercimek Corbasi?**
A: Store cooled soup in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. To reheat, gently warm it on the stovetop over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally. You might need to add a splash of water or broth to thin it out, as it can thicken in the fridge. It also freezes well for up to 3 months.

**Q: Why isn't my soup creamy?**
A: The most common reasons for a non-creamy soup are not blending enough or not simmering the lentils long enough. Ensure the lentils are very soft before blending, and use an immersion blender or standard blender to get a completely smooth, velvety texture.

**Q: What kind of lentils should I use for Mercimek Corbasi?**
A: For authentic Mercimek Corbasi, use split red lentils (sometimes called masoor dal). They cook quickly and break down completely, which is essential for the soup's characteristic smooth, creamy texture. Whole red lentils or other lentil varieties won't give the same result.

Embrace the warmth and flavor of Turkish cuisine by trying your hand at this delightful Mercimek Corbasi. It's a dish that promises comfort, nourishment, and a truly authentic taste of Turkey with every spoonful.
